subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV 2025, OECD, World Justice Project, RSF) + strong German official data (Destatis, Bundesbank, BKA, BMI, Deutsche Bahn, GKV) + German press.
The economy is in structural (not cyclical) industrial stress; the 2025 debt-brake reform and a EUR 500B infrastructure/defence fund are the deliberate upside catalyst.
Source-lean: moderate Anglophone routing on political/social color; the underlying numbers come from BKA/Destatis primaries.

economic.notes: A strong fiscal/monetary anchor, but the real economy is in structural stress; the 2025 fiscal pivot is the upside catalyst.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~63% GDP (low among large peers), but the March 2025 debt-brake reform plus a EUR 500B infrastructure/defence fund and a defence-spending exemption push the deficit toward ~4% and debt toward ~68% by 2027 - a deliberate expansion. (IMF Art IV 2025; Bundesfinanzministerium)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible and independent; no national control, with the Bundesbank a leading voice. (ECB, Bundesbank)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: 2025 average ~2.2%, ~2.9% (Apr 2026) on an energy/fuel spike; the Bundesbank sees ~2% by 2027-28. (Destatis, Bundesbank)

banking_stability.notes: Banks adequately capitalised and liquid; the Bundesbank flags elevated commercial-real-estate credit risk requiring monitoring. (Bundesbank FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: An export/industrial powerhouse in structural stress: manufacturing output down three straight years, autos/machinery losing share to China, high energy costs post-Russian-gas, and US tariff exposure; near-flat GDP after two recession years. (Destatis; IMF)

institutional.notes: A top-tier baseline (independent courts and statistics) with a mild rule-of-law erosion trend and rising journalist threats.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP #6/143; an independent Federal Constitutional Court; security-law expansions a watch-item. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Destatis is legally independent and Eurostat-compliant. (Destatis, Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable and rule-bound but notoriously slow to digitalize, with federal fragmentation across 16 Laender. (OECD 2025)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~#14 (2026); attacks on journalists roughly doubled, with security-law concerns. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A functioning grand coalition, against deep polarization, a surging hard right, and ageing demographics.

political_stability.notes: The 2021 coalition collapsed in late 2024; a Feb 2025 election produced a CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition under Merz; the hard-right AfD doubled to ~21% and leads some polls. Governable but fragmented and polarized. (CRS)

civil_liberties.notes: A strong baseline; asylum/migration restrictions and surveillance expansion test equal-treatment norms. (WJP, RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: The skilled path stays open and lawful (the 2024 points-based Opportunity Card, EU Blue Card, freelance visa; dual citizenship retained, naturalization at 5 years), but late-2025 repealed the three-year fast track, suspended some family reunification, and added border turn-backs. Open for skilled, hardening overall. (BMI; OECD Migration Outlook 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: A record ~84,000 politically motivated crimes in 2024 (+40%, far-right the largest share) and rising antisemitic and asylum-shelter attacks; a high-trust baseline cushions it. (BKA 2024)

demographics.notes: An ageing population with low fertility; labour-force growth is migration-dependent, with pension/health-spending pressure. (Destatis, IMF)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable NATO/EU anchor mid-rearmament; tail exposure via Russia, no direct conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU core member mid-rearmament (Zeitenwende; defence budget rising sharply, committed to 5% by 2035). (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: A major Ukraine backer with Russian hybrid/sabotage exposure; no direct conflict. (NATO; Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime; free capital mobility, no domestic controls. (EU, Bundesbank)

physical_practical.notes: Strong healthcare, against notably degraded infrastructure (now being funded for repair) and a flood-protection gap.

climate.notes: Rising extreme weather (the 2021 Ahr flood, heatwaves); an energy transition underway post-Russian-gas. (UBA)

healthcare.notes: Universal statutory insurance with strong access and outcomes, but financial strain (a large projected deficit, rising contributions) needing reform. (GKV-Spitzenverband, OECD)

infrastructure.notes: Notably degraded - poor rail punctuality and a 2024 bridge collapse symptomatic of decay - but the EUR 500B fund targets a turnaround. Degraded now, funded recovery ahead. (Deutsche Bahn; Railway Gazette)

crime_safety.notes: Generally safe (a low completed-homicide rate) with localized concerns. (BKA 2024)

disaster_insurance.notes: Strong response capacity, but only ~54% of homes carry flood cover; a mandatory-insurance debate is unresolved. (GDV)

summary: A low-debt fiscal anchor now deliberately expanding (the 2025 debt-brake reform and a EUR 500B infrastructure/defence fund), top-tier institutions and statistics, strong universal healthcare, and a reliable NATO/EU role. The drags are an export/industrial economy in structural stress (autos, energy costs, China competition), deep political polarization with a surging hard right, notably degraded infrastructure (now targeted for repair), and ageing demographics.
